title: [ipv6_addr.c:28-33] LOG macro fallbacks are no-ops, could hide errors
description:
Gap found: Logging abstraction in non-Zephyr builds not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Host test infrastructure not examined.

The actual bug: Lines 28-33 define LOG_ERR, LOG_WRN, LOG_INF, LOG_DBG as ((void)0) for non-Zephyr builds. This allows the module to compile for host-side unit tests.

The issue: if a host test triggers an error condition (e.g., NULL pointer), the LOG_ERR is silently discarded. Tests won't see the error message.

Solutions:
1. Use fprintf(stderr, ...) in non-Zephyr builds
2. Use assert() for critical errors in test builds
3. Provide a test hook to capture log messages

This is a test infrastructure quality issue, not a production bug. The production (Zephyr) code logs correctly.

Priority 4 - test infrastructure improvement.
close_reason: Fixed: Changed LOG_ERR/LOG_WRN fallbacks from no-ops to fprintf(stderr) for host test visibility
